questions.question: Which keyboard shortcut creates a new link to another page in Notion?

options.text: Press the opening bracket key twice ( [[ )
options.explanation: In Notion, typing [[ opens the link dialog, letting you select a page to link to.

options.text: Press Ctrl + K
options.explanation: Ctrl + K opens the link dialog for external URLs, not for internal page links.

options.text: Press the slash key (/)
options.explanation: The slash key opens the block menu, not the link creation interface.

options.text: Press the at symbol (@)
options.explanation: The @ symbol mentions people or dates, not pages.

questions.question: What happens automatically when you change a page’s title after linking to it elsewhere?

options.text: All links to that page update to show the new title
options.explanation: Notion links are dynamic; they reflect any title or icon changes across the workspace.

options.text: Only the original page’s title changes; links remain unchanged
options.explanation: Links do not stay static; they update with the page’s new title.

options.text: Links break and must be recreated manually
options.explanation: Links do not break when a title changes; Notion handles the update automatically.

options.text: The page’s icon changes, but the title stays the same in links
options.explanation: Both title and icon updates propagate to linked instances.

questions.question: Where does Notion place the automatically generated backlink when you create a link?

options.text: At the top of the linked page
options.explanation: Backlinks appear at the top of the page you linked to, showing where it was referenced.

options.text: In the sidebar navigation
options.explanation: Backlinks are not shown in the sidebar; they appear within the page content.

options.text: At the bottom of the linked page
options.explanation: Backlinks are not placed at the bottom; they are shown at the top for quick reference.

options.text: In a separate “References” database
options.explanation: Notion does not create a separate database for backlinks; they are inline on the page.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of backlinks in a Notion workspace?

options.text: They act as an automatically generated “references” section, helping discover related pages
options.explanation: Backlinks provide a built‑in reference list, making it easy to see which pages mention the current page.

options.text: They increase the page load speed
options.explanation: Backlinks have no effect on performance; they are for navigation and context.

options.text: They allow you to edit the linked page directly from the backlink
options.explanation: Clicking a backlink navigates to the original page; it does not enable inline editing.

options.text: They automatically translate page content into other languages
options.explanation: Backlinks are not a translation feature.

questions.question: If you click a backlink on a page, where does Notion take you?

options.text: To the exact location in the source page where it was mentioned
options.explanation: Backlinks jump to the specific part of the source page that contains the link reference.

options.text: To the top of the source page
options.explanation: Notion scrolls to the precise mention, not just the top of the page.

options.text: To a random page in the workspace
options.explanation: Backlinks are deterministic; they always lead to the linked context.

options.text: To the Notion help center
options.explanation: Backlinks stay within your own workspace, not external resources.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about Notion links is FALSE?

options.text: Links automatically update when the linked page’s title changes
options.explanation: This is true; Notion’s links are dynamic.

options.text: Links can be created by typing [[ and selecting a page
options.explanation: This is the correct method for internal page links.

options.text: Links are static and must be manually edited after any page changes
options.explanation: This is false; links are dynamic and update automatically.

options.text: Backlinks appear at the top of the referenced page
options.explanation: Backlinks do appear at the top, so this statement is true.

questions.question: How can you explore more ways to use links and backlinks in Notion beyond this guide?

options.text: Browse the template gallery for examples
options.explanation: The guide suggests checking the template gallery for additional use cases.

options.text: Enable your ad blocker to see the hidden video
options.explanation: The ad blocker blocks the video; disabling it, not enabling, would help, but the guide points to external resources.

options.text: Subscribe to the Notion YouTube channel
options.explanation: While useful, the guide specifically mentions the template gallery as a place to explore.

options.text: Contact Notion support via email
options.explanation: The guide does not list email support for this purpose.

questions.question: What is the main reason Notion recommends using backlinks as you “grow your workspace”?

options.text: They help you quickly discover related pages without manually maintaining a references list
options.explanation: Backlinks automatically show where a page is mentioned, acting as a dynamic reference system as the workspace expands.

options.text: They increase the number of pages you can create
options.explanation: Backlinks do not affect page limits.

options.text: They automatically backup your workspace to the cloud
options.explanation: Backlinks are a navigation feature, not a backup mechanism.

options.text: They replace the need for tags or databases
options.explanation: While helpful, backlinks complement, not replace, other organization tools like tags or databases.
